# Command-Line Help for `fmf`

This document contains the help content for the `fmf` command-line program.

* [`fmf`‚Ü¥](#fmf)

## `fmf`

find-my-files engine developer CLI

**Usage:** `fmf [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>`

###### **Subcommands:**

* `spike` ‚Äî Spike S0: scan a volume's $MFT and print raw measurements
* `index` ‚Äî Build the index for a volume, print stats, then run an interactive query REPL (requires an elevated terminal)
* `bench` ‚Äî Index a volume and run the fixed benchmark query set
* `stats` ‚Äî Index a volume and dump per-column memory accounting as JSON
* `io-probe` ‚Äî Measure $MFT read throughput per I/O strategy (elevated terminal; reads the scan's exact chunk plan, parses nothing). Verdicts live in docs/RESEARCH.md ‚Äî production reads stay buffered until a mode wins
* `diag` ‚Äî Print versions, log locations and the in-process diagnostics ring
* `watch` ‚Äî Index a volume, then tail its USN journal and apply changes live, printing one line per applied batch (Ctrl+C to stop)
* `criterion-gate` ‚Äî Gate criterion micro-bench results: scan change reports written by `cargo bench -- --baseline <name>` and exit 1 past the threshold (criterion itself never sets an exit code on regressions)

###### **Options:**

* `--color <COLOR>` ‚Äî When to colourise human-facing output (auto: only on a terminal)

  Default value: `auto`

  Possible values:
  - `auto`:
    Colour when the stream is a terminal and the environment allows it
  - `always`:
    Always emit colour, even when redirected
  - `never`:
    Never emit colour

* `-q`, `--quiet` ‚Äî Suppress the progress spinner and other stderr chrome
* `--format <FORMAT>` ‚Äî Output format. `json` emits a machine-readable document on stdout for the commands that support it (diag, bench, watch); others stay text

  Default value: `human`

  Possible values:
  - `human`:
    Human-readable text (tables, colour, the progress spinner)
  - `json`:
    A single machine-readable JSON document on stdout (NDJSON ‚Äî one object per line ‚Äî for streaming commands like `watch`). Not every command has a JSON form; those keep printing text




## `fmf spike`

Spike S0: scan a volume's $MFT and print raw measurements

**Usage:** `fmf spike <DRIVE>`

###### **Arguments:**

* `<DRIVE>`



## `fmf index`

Build the index for a volume, print stats, then run an interactive query REPL (requires an elevated terminal)

**Usage:** `fmf index [OPTIONS] <DRIVE>`

###### **Arguments:**

* `<DRIVE>`

###### **Options:**

* `--stats` ‚Äî Print stats and exit without the REPL
* `--include-hidden-system` ‚Äî Include hidden/system entries (excluded by default, like the app)



## `fmf bench`

Index a volume and run the fixed benchmark query set

**Usage:** `fmf bench [OPTIONS] <DRIVE>`

###### **Arguments:**

* `<DRIVE>`

###### **Options:**

* `--json <JSON>` ‚Äî Write the full report as JSON
* `--baseline <BASELINE>` ‚Äî Compare against a previous --json report; exit 1 when p50 or p99 regress by more than 20%



## `fmf stats`

Index a volume and dump per-column memory accounting as JSON

**Usage:** `fmf stats [OPTIONS] <DRIVE>`

###### **Arguments:**

* `<DRIVE>`

###### **Options:**

* `--trigram-estimate` ‚Äî Also estimate what a trigram index would cost on this volume's real names ‚Äî input to the n-gram go/no-go criteria in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md (read-only, nothing is built)
* `--name-stats` ‚Äî Also dump per-name statistics over the live entries (fold identity, duplication, length distribution, ‚â•4GiB sizes) ‚Äî the measured inputs for pool/column layout decisions (read-only)



## `fmf io-probe`

Measure $MFT read throughput per I/O strategy (elevated terminal; reads the scan's exact chunk plan, parses nothing). Verdicts live in docs/RESEARCH.md ‚Äî production reads stay buffered until a mode wins

**Usage:** `fmf io-probe [OPTIONS] <DRIVE>`

###### **Arguments:**

* `<DRIVE>`

###### **Options:**

* `--mode <MODE>`

  Default value: `buffered`

  Possible values: `buffered`, `seq`, `nobuf`, `nobuf-ov`

* `--qd <QD>` ‚Äî Outstanding reads for nobuf-ov

  Default value: `4`
* `--runs <RUNS>`

  Default value: `3`



## `fmf diag`

Print versions, log locations and the in-process diagnostics ring

**Usage:** `fmf diag`



## `fmf watch`

Index a volume, then tail its USN journal and apply changes live, printing one line per applied batch (Ctrl+C to stop)

**Usage:** `fmf watch <DRIVE>`

###### **Arguments:**

* `<DRIVE>`



## `fmf criterion-gate`

Gate criterion micro-bench results: scan change reports written by `cargo bench -- --baseline <name>` and exit 1 past the threshold (criterion itself never sets an exit code on regressions)

**Usage:** `fmf criterion-gate [OPTIONS]`

###### **Options:**

* `--dir <DIR>` ‚Äî Criterion output directory

  Default value: `target/criterion`
* `--threshold <THRESHOLD>` ‚Äî Relative median regression threshold (0.10 = +10%)

  Default value: `0.1`
